name: demo_sh
description: Demonstrate how to create a tool using Bash and how to use comment tags.

string_enum.description: Define a required string property with enum

array.description: Define a required string array property

array_optional.description: Define a optional string array property

name: execute_command
description: Execute the shell command.

name: execute_js_code
description: Execute the javascript code in node.js.

code.description: Javascript code to execute, such as `console.log("hello world")`

name: execute_sql_code
description: Execute the sql code.

name: fetch_url_via_curl
description: Extract the content from a given URL.

name: fetch_url_via_jina
description: Extract the content from a given URL.

name: fs_cat
description:
Read the contents of a file at the specified path.
Use this when you need to examine the contents of an existing file.

path.description: The path of the file to read

name: fs_ls
description: List all files and directories at the specified path.

path.description: The path of the directory to list

name: fs_mkdir
description: Create a new directory at the specified path.

path.description: The path of the directory to create

name: fs_patch
description:
Apply a patch to a file at the specified path.
This can be used to edit the file, without having to rewrite the whole file.

path.description: The path of the file to apply to

contents.description:
The patch to apply to the file

Here is an example of a patch block that can be applied to modify the file to request the user's name:
--- a/hello.py
+++ b/hello.py
@@ ... @@
 def hello():
-    print("Hello World")
+    name = input("What is your name? ")
+    print(f"Hello {name}")

name: fs_rm
description: Remove the file or directory at the specified path.

path.description: The path of the file or directory to remove

name: fs_write
description: Write the full file contents to a file at the specified path.

path.description: The path of the file to write to

contents.description: The full contents to write to the file

name: fs_mv
description: Move or rename a file or directory.

source.description: The source path of the file or directory.

name: fs_cp
description: Copy a file or directory.

source.description: The source path of the file or directory.

recursive.description: Boolean to indicate recursive copy for directories.

name: fs_organize
description: Organize files by moving them into target directories based on patterns or extensions.

source_dir.description: The source directory containing files to organize.

target_dir.description: The target directory to move files to.

name: delegate_to_workflow
description: Delegate a complex, multi-step request to the Workflow Agent.

request.description: The full request string to delegate.

name: calendar_check_conflicts
description: Check for calendar conflicts within a given time range.

name: calendar_find_free_slots
description: Find free time slots on a given date.

duration_minutes.description: The required duration of the free slot.

start_hour.description: The start of the working day (default 9).

end_hour.description: The end of the working day (default 18).

name: sync_tasks_reminders
description: Sync open SQLite tasks with Apple Reminders.

name: sync_projects_bidirectional
description: Bidirectional sync between project Markdown notes and SQLite assistant database.

name: vision_analyze_organize
description: Use vision analysis to categorize and organize a file into project or research assets.

file_path.description: The path of the image file to organize.

target_type.description: The explicit target type (project or research).

name: deep_research
description: Perform a deep, multi-turn autonomous research investigation.

name: get_current_time
description: Get the current time.

name: get_current_weather
description: Get the current weather in a given location.

location.description: The city and optionally the state or country, e.g., "London", "San Francisco, CA".

name: search_arxiv
description: Search arXiv for a query and return the top papers.

name: search_wikipedia
description:
Search Wikipedia for a query.
Uses it to get detailed information about a public figure, interpretation of a complex scientific concept or in-depth connectivity of a significant historical event,.

name: search_wolframalpha
description:
Get an answer to a question using Wolfram Alpha. Input should the query in English.
Use it to answer user questions that require computation, detailed facts, data analysis, or complex queries.

name: send_mail
description: Send a email.

name: send_twilio
description: Send SMS or Twilio Messaging Channels messages using Twilio API.

to_number.description: The recipient's phone number. Prefix with 'whatsapp:' for WhatsApp messages, e.g. whatsapp:+1234567890

message.description: The content of the message to be sent

name: web_search_aichat
description:
Perform a web search to get up-to-date information or additional context.
Use this when you need current information or feel a search could provide a better answer.

name: web_search_perplexity
description:
Perform a web search using Perplexity API to get up-to-date information or additional context.
Use this when you need current information or feel a search could provide a better answer.

name: web_search_tavily
description:
Perform a web search using Tavily API to get up-to-date information or additional context.
Use this when you need current information or feel a search could provide a better answer.

name: workspace_map
description: Analyze a project directory to identify its structure, tech stack, and entry points.

dir_path.description: The root path of the project to map.

name: autonomous_fix
description: Autonomous debugging loop: Run a command, capture error, fix code, and repeat.

test_command.description: The command to execute (e.g., "npm test" or "python script.py").

name: browser_get_active_tab
description: Get the URL and title of the active tab in Safari or Chrome.

browser.description: The browser to check (safari or chrome). Defaults to safari.

name: file_find
description: Find files by name pattern or extension.

name: file_classify
description: Classify a list of files to suggest target directories and descriptive names.

files.description: Comma-separated list of filenames or paths.

context_dirs.description: Comma-separated list of potential target directories.
